message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking for summary feature work

Updates bead status to reflect current implementation progress.

## Status Changes

ntm-srrl: "Implement activity summary generation"
  Status: open → in_progress
  Updated: 2026-01-06T11:28:52

  This bead tracks the activity summary feature being implemented.
  Core implementation (synthesis.go) and CLI integration (summary.go,
  root.go) are now committed. Feature is partially complete - needs
  testing and potential refinements before closing.

ntm-stjt: "Create ntm conflicts and ntm diff commands"
  Status: open → closed
  Closed: 2026-01-06T11:24:02
  Reason: "Commands already implemented: ntm conflicts in changes.go
          and ntm diff in diff.go. The functionality requested
          already exists."

  Investigation revealed these commands were already implemented in
  previous work. Closing as duplicate/already-done.

## Context

These beads are part of the Output Synthesis epic (ntm-sp0c) which
covers comparing, diffing, and synthesizing outputs from parallel
agents. The epic includes:
- File conflict detection (ntm-1m1m) - complete
- Output capture with tagging (ntm-iye0) - complete
- Robot-diff API (ntm-1bke) - complete
- Activity summary (ntm-srrl) - in progress
- Conflicts/diff commands (ntm-stjt) - already existed, closed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-sor1","title":"Feature: File Reservation Lifecycle","description":"# File Reservation Lifecycle

## The Problem
NTM spawns multiple agents on the same codebase with NO file coordination. Result: merge conflicts, lost work, frustrated developers.

## The Solution
Advisory file locks via Agent Mail:
1. RESERVE before assigning work
2. RENEW heartbeat while working
3. RELEASE after completion
4. FORCE-RELEASE if agent crashes (with approval)

## Lifecycle
```
Reserve → Work → Release
    ↓
  Conflict? → Find alternative work OR wait
    ↓
  Stale? → Force-release (requires approval)
```

## Implementation

### Reserve Before Assignment
```go
func assignWorkWithReservations(ctx, session, agent, bead) {
    files := predictAffectedFiles(bead)
    reservations := agentmail.ReservePaths(ctx, files)
    if len(reservations.Conflicts) > 0 {
        // Find alternative work or wait
    }
    // Assign work
}
```

### Predict Affected Files
Use bv --robot-impact if available, otherwise use label-to-pattern mapping.

### Release After Completion
```go
func OnTaskComplete(ctx, session, agentName) {
    agentmail.ReleaseReservations(ctx, projectPath, agentName)
}
```

### Force-Release Stale (with Approval)
```go
func checkForStaleReservations(ctx) {
    for _, res := range reservations {
        if time.Since(agent.LastActiveTS) > staleTimeout {
            approval := policyChecker.CheckApproval(ctx, "force_release", res.ID)
            if approval.Granted {
                agentmail.ForceReleaseReservation(ctx, res.ID)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Pre-Commit Guards
```bash
ntm guards install    # Install Agent Mail pre-commit hook
ntm guards status     # Check hook status
```

## New Commands
```bash
ntm reserve "internal/auth/**/*.go" --agent=GreenLake --ttl=1h
ntm release --agent=GreenLake
ntm reservations list
ntm reservations force-release <id> --reason="Agent crashed"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reserve-before-assign implemented
- [ ] Release-after-complete implemented
- [ ] Force-release with approval workflow
- [ ] Pre-commit guard installation
- [ ] Conflicts detected and handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-03T20:16:40.737974-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T12:53:26.805914-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T12:53:26.805914-05:00","close_reason":"File Reservation Lifecycle implemented: ntm lock/unlock/locks commands with force-release and renew subcommands. -{"id":"ntm-srrl","title":"Implement activity summary generation","description":"Generate per-agent activity summaries. Provides overview of what each agent accomplished.

PURPOSE:
After parallel agent work, users need a quick summary of:
- What did each agent work on?
- What files were modified?
- How much output was generated?
- Were there any errors?

SUMMARY DATA:
```go
type ActivitySummary struct {
    Session      string
    TimeRange    TimeRange
    Agents       []AgentSummary
    TotalFiles   int
    Conflicts    []FileConflict
}

type AgentSummary struct {
    PaneID       string
    AgentType    string
    
    // Activity metrics
    ActiveTime   time.Duration  // time in GENERATING state
    IdleTime     time.Duration  // time in WAITING state
    OutputLines  int
    OutputChars  int
    
    // Content summary
    FilesModified []string
    CodeBlocks    int           // count of code blocks produced
    Commands      int           // count of commands run
    
    // Health
    Errors       int
    Restarts     int
    
    // Highlights (extracted from output)
    KeyActions   []string       // e.g., "Created src/auth.go", "Fixed test"
}
```

KEY ACTION EXTRACTION:
Heuristics to identify key actions from output:
- Lines containing: "Created", "Modified", "Fixed", "Added", "Removed"
- Lines with file paths + action verbs
- Summary sections ("Changes made:", "Summary:")
- Limit to top 5 key actions per agent

OUTPUT FORMATS:

1. HUMAN READABLE:
```
Session: myproject (last 30 minutes)

Agent myproject__cc_1 (Claude):
  Active: 12m 30s | Output: 2,450 lines
  Files: src/auth.go, src/user.go, tests/auth_test.go
  Key actions:
    - Created authentication middleware
    - Added JWT token validation
    - Fixed password hashing

Agent myproject__cc_2 (Claude):
  Active: 8m 15s | Output: 1,200 lines
  Files: src/api/routes.go
  Key actions:
    - Added user registration endpoint
```

2. JSON (for robot mode)
Full structured data as above.

CLI COMMAND:
```bash
ntm summary SESSION [--since=30m] [--format=text|json]
```

ROBOT MODE:
```bash
ntm --robot-summary=SESSION --since=30m
```

LOCATION: internal/robot/synthesis.go

DEPENDS ON:
- Output capture with tagging (ntm-iye0)
- File conflict detection (ntm-1m1m)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Summary generated for each active agent
- Metrics calculated correctly
- Key actions extracted from output
- Both text and JSON formats work
- Time range filtering works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:09.155958446-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:09.155958446-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-srrl","depends_on_id":"ntm-iye0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:18.516174628-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ntm-srrl","depends_on_id":"ntm-1m1m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:45:18.58044592-06:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-t0ch","title":"Implement file conflict detection","description":"# Implement File Conflict Detection

## Purpose

Detect when multiple agents modify the same file, potentially creating conflicts.

## Detection Logic

```go
type Conflict struct {
    Path   string
    Agents []AgentChange
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []AgentChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := make(map[string][]AgentChange)
    for _, c := range changes {
        byPath[c.File] = append(byPath[c.File], c)
    }
    
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{
                Path:   path,
                Agents: agents,
            })
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## Severity Levels

- **Warning**: Same file modified by 2+ agents (may or may not conflict)
- **Critical**: Same lines modified (if we can detect via git diff)

## Integration with ntm send --all

After broadcast completes:
```
ntm send myproject --all "Implement the feature"
# ... agents work ...
# On completion:
⚠️ Potential conflict: src/api/users.py modified by cc_1, cc_2
  Run `ntm conflicts myproject` for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Detect same-file modifications across agents
- [ ] Warn user after broadcasts
- [ ] ntm conflicts command shows details
- [ ] Severity classification (warning vs critical)","notes":"Picking up highest-priority free task; conflict detection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:14.387332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T21:21:36.565175-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T21:21:36.565175-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-t0ch","depends_on_id":"ntm-xkvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:37.60962-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
